capabilities: Deployment topology must match the desired architecture to ensure system reliability and performance.

procedure.action: Extract current deployment topology using infrastructure as code tools like Terraform or AWS CloudFormation; output should be in a structured format such as JSON or YAML using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Diff extracted topology against the desired architecture blueprint using a configuration management tool like Ansible; identify discrepancies in service placement and resource allocation by computing diff(A, B) on each shared field; assert abs(A.value - B.value) < tolerance_threshold; report comparison_delta_count and max_delta_observed.

Render a report of mismatches by running a diff tool on the current and desired configurations; highlight differences in node count, instance types, and network settings using deterministic template rendering with seeded RNG; assert output matches expected schema before persisting; assert generation_error_count < 1 and output_size_bytes > 0.

Execute necessary configuration changes using infrastructure as code scripts to align the current deployment with the desired architecture; ensure changes are version-controlled by executing the transformation function on each input record; assert output_schema_valid = true and error_count < 1; log transformation_duration_ms.
Revalidate the updated deployment topology by rerunning the extraction and comparison steps; confirm that all discrepancies have been resolved and the topology matches the blueprint.

summary: Ensure deployment topology aligns with desired architecture by comparing configurations and adjusting discrepancies.
title: Deployment Topology Validation

failureConditions: Deployment drift occurs when topology changes are not tracked, causing misalignment with the desired architecture and potential performance degradation.
Resource misallocation happens when discrepancies in instance types or node counts are not corrected, leading to inefficient resource usage and increased costs.
Network misconfiguration arises when network settings differ from the blueprint, causing connectivity issues and potential security vulnerabilities.
metrics: output matches expected schema before persisting
successConditions: run terraform plan; assert no changes required; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
run ansible-playbook --check; assert no tasks to be applied; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
diff current_topology.json desired_topology.json; assert output is empty; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
